title: ranunculus nemorosus subsp. delacourii
intro: Ranunculus nemorosus subsp. delacourii, also known as Delacour's Ranunculus, is a lesser-known subspecies of the Ranunculus nemorosus plant, native to China and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparation of Ranunculus nemorosus subsp. delacourii typically involves drying the plant parts, followed by infusion or decoction to make a tea or other medicinal concoctions.
